content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 16, 1985

John Stremple, Superintendent
Fresno Unified School District
Tulare and M Streets
Fresno, CA 93721

Re: Our File No. A-85-237

Dear Mr. Stremple:

This is in response to your letter of November 6, 1985, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015). It is my understanding that pursuant to a telephone conversation between Nancy Smith of the County Counsel's office and Robert Leidigh of the Commission's legal staff, your request for a formal opinion has been changed to a request for written advice under FPPC regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329.

Your letter asked whether the Fresno Unified School District had made a "contribution," as defined in Government Code Section 82015, to the Fresno Teachers Association by allowing the union to use the district's internal mail system to distribute material endorsing three school board candidates.

Because the union regularly uses the district's internal mail system to communicate with its members pursuant to a collective bargaining agreement between the union and the district, the union's use of the internal mail system to distribute mail endorsing candidates would not constitute a contribution from the district to the union. This advice would not apply, of course, if some other entity, such as a corporation, allowed the union to use its internal mail system to distribute such materials.

I hope the foregoing has been helpful. Please let me know if I can be of any assistance in the future.

Sincerely,

Carla Wardlow
Political Reform Consultant

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

BOARD OF EDUCATION
Darlene M. Laval, President
Ruth M. Gadebusch, Clerk
Keith Chun
Dorothy Rohlfing
William Stegall

SUPERINTENDENT OF SCHOOLS
John L. Stremple, Ed.D.

FRESNO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
EDUCATION CENTER
TULARE AND M STREETS
FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93721
TELEPHONE (209) 441-3000

OFFICE OF THE SUPERINTENDENT
William A. Hansen
Assistant to the Superintendent
Management Services

Gordon Graves
Director
Employer-Employee Relations

November 6, 1985

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 700
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

I am writing to request a formal opinion from your office
regarding the use by the Fresno Teachers Association of the
union members' mailboxes maintained by the Fresno Unified
School District.

QUESTION PRESENTED

The specific question is: does the district's allowing the
union to use the internal mail system to distribute the
enclosed material constitute a "contribution" for purposes of
Government Code Section 82015?

BACKGROUND

Government Code Section 3543.1 Subdivision (b) provides in
part that employee organizations shall have the right to use
institutional mailboxes and other means of communication. In
addition, the collective bargaining agreement currently in
effect between Fresno Unified School District and the Fresno
Teachers Association/CTA/NEA provides in Article 3 Section 2 in
part:

"The association may use the district mail
service and teacher mailboxes for
communication to teachers."

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page Two
November 6, 1985

The attached material was sent through the district mail
service to union members in October, 1985. A number of
candidates for the school board have complained to the district
regarding the use of the mailboxes by the union to support
certain candidates. Their allegation apparently is that the
district has made a contribution within the meaning of
Government Code Section 82015 to the union and should have the
obligation to report that contribution to the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

Ms. Nancy Smith, Deputy County Counsel, contacted your
office on our behalf on October 24, 1985 and spoke to Ms. Carla
Wardlow. Ms. Wardlow informed Ms. Smith, after discussing the
matter with the head of your legal department, that there was
no contribution from the district to the union that would
require reporting since it was the regular policy of the
district to let the union use the internal mail system and
since the district did not take any different action regarding
this union mailing than it had taken with the other
distributions of the mail by the union to the union members.
This conclusion would be the same, Ms. Wardlow indicated,
regardless of whether the political message was contained in
the regular union newsletter or whether it was a special
distribution just for the purpose of getting out the union's
message regarding an election.

That information was given to the public at the regular
board meeting of the Fresno Unified School District Board of
Education on October 24, 1985. Apparently not satisfied with
that information, a Mr. Paul Winter on behalf of Mr. John
Segura, a candidate for the Board of Education, contacted
County Counsel, indicating that he had spoken to Ms. Lynn
Montgomery of your office and that Ms. Montgomery had informed
him that in fact there was a violation of the Political Reform
Act. Upon checking with Ms. Montgomery, she indicated that she
had not spoken to Mr. Winter and had given no opinion to the
effect that there was a violation.

As you can see then, with the conflicting oral reports we
have heard it would be most advantageous for us to have a
written opinion from your office that we could share with
interested candidates and with the public at large and to
insure that the district can be certain of the appropriateness
of its actions.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page Three
November 6, 1985


I would appreciate your earliest response to my request.
If you require any further information please feel free to
contact Ms. Nancy Smith directly at the Fresno County Counsel's
Office. Thank you for your attention to my request.

Very truly yours,

JOHN STREMPLE, Superintendent
FRESNO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT

JS:NIS:ap
5422A

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


November 19, 1985


John Stremple, Superintendent
Fresno Unified School District
Education Center
Tulare and M Streets
Fresno, CA  93721

Re:  A-85-237

Dear Mr. Stremple:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been referred to a consultant in the Technical
Assistance and Analysis Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this person directly at
(916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

CALIFORNIA STATE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION                                Bill Honig

721 Capitol Mall                                                              Superintendent

Sacramento, CA 95814-4785                                                    of Public Instruction


                                                                DEC 13 2 05 PM '85


December 11, 1985



Robert D. Segura
5716 N. Fourth Street
Fresno, CA  93710

Dear Mr. Segura:

Superintendent Honig has referred your letter to me for reply.  Education Code
Sections 7050-7057, enclosed, relate to Political Activities of School Officers
and Employees. While these sections do give to the governing board of your
district a certain amount of authority to establish rules and regulations
regarding the political activities of employees during working hours, Section
7054 specifically prohibits the use of school district funds, services,
supplies, or equipment to urge the candidacy of any person for election to the
governing board of the district. We are not, however, the legal advisers to
your district.  Further questions should be addressed to the district's
counsel.

The governing board of your school district has the authority to resolve this
matter. Your complaints regarding the contents of the teachers' agreement
itself may be lodged with the Public Employment Relations Board, 1031 18th
Street, Sacramento, CA 95814.

Sincerely,

Joseph R. Symkowick
Chief Counsel
(916) 445-4694
